subject: Math
description: Understand statistics as a process for making inferences to be made about population parameters based on a random sample from that population.
description_plain: Your child understands that statistics help make guesses about a whole group based on a random sample.

subject: Math
description: Distinguish between correlation and causation.
description_plain: Your child understands the difference between things that happen together and things where one causes the other.

subject: Math
description: Compute (using technology) and interpret the correlation coefficient of a linear fit.
description_plain: Your child can use technology to find and explain how closely two things are related in a straight-line pattern.

subject: Math
description: Fit quadratic and exponential functions to the data. Use functions fitted to data to solve problems in the context of the data.
description_plain: Your child can fit quadratic and exponential functions to data and use them to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Assess the fit of a function by plotting and analyzing residuals.
description_plain: Your child can check how well a function fits data by looking at the differences between the data and the function.

subject: Math
description: Use the mean and standard deviation of a data set to fit it to a normal distribution and to estimate population percentages. Recognize that there are data sets for which such a procedure is not appropriate. Use calculators, spreadsheets, and tables to estimate areas under the normal curve.
description_plain: Your child can use the average and spread of data to fit it to a normal curve and estimate percentages, knowing when this method works best.

subject: Math
description: Derive the equations of ellipses and hyperbolas given the foci, using the fact that the sum or difference of distances from the foci is constant; graph the ellipse or hyperbola in the coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can find and graph equations for ellipses and hyperbolas using their special distance properties.

subject: Math
description: Derive the equation of a parabola given a focus and directrix; graph the parabola in the coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can find and graph the equation of a parabola using its focus and directrix.

subject: Math
description: Complete the square to find the center and radius of a circle given by an equation.
description_plain: Your child can rewrite a circle’s equation to find its center and radius.

subject: Math
description: Derive the equation of a circle of given center and radius using the Pythagorean Theorem; graph the circle in the coordinate plane;
description_plain: Your child can write and graph the equation of a circle using its center and radius.

subject: Math
description: Derive using similarity the fact that the length of the arc intercepted by an angle is proportional to the radius, and define the radian measure of the angle as the constant of proportionality; derive the formula for the area of a sector.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the length of an arc relates to the radius and angle, define radians, and find the area of a sector.

subject: Math
description: Construct inscribed and circumscribed circles for polygons and tangent lines from a point outside a given circle to the circle.
description_plain: Your child can draw circles inside and outside polygons and draw tangent lines from a point outside a circle.

subject: Math
description: Construct inscribed and circumscribed circles for triangles.
description_plain: Your child can draw circles inside and outside triangles.

subject: Math
description: Understand and apply the Law of Sines and the Law of Cosines to find unknown measurements in right and non-right triangles (e.g. surveying problems, resultant forces).
description_plain: Your child can use the Law of Sines and Law of Cosines to find missing parts of any triangle.

subject: Math
description: Prove the Laws of Sines and Cosines and use them to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can prove the Law of Sines and Law of Cosines and use them to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Derive the formula A = ½ ab sin C for the area of a triangle by drawing an auxiliary line from a vertex perpendicular to the opposite side.
description_plain: Your child can find the formula for the area of a triangle using a line drawn inside the triangle.

subject: Math
description: Use congruence and similarity criteria for triangles to solve problems and to prove relationships in geometric figures.
description_plain: Your child can use rules about when triangles are the same shape or size to solve problems and explain how parts of shapes relate.

subject: Math
description: Construct arguments about triangles using theorems. Theorems include: a line parallel to one side of a triangle divides the other two proportionally, and conversely; the Pythagorean Theorem proved using triangle similarity, and AA.
description_plain: Your child can explain why certain lines and triangles have special properties using important triangle rules like the Pythagorean Theorem.

subject: Math
description: Construct an equilateral triangle, a square, and a regular hexagon inscribed in a circle.
description_plain: Your child can draw an equilateral triangle, a square, and a regular hexagon inside a circle.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate triangle congruence using rigid motion (ASA, SAS, and SSS).
description_plain: Your child can show that two triangles are exactly the same by moving them without changing their shape or size.

subject: Math
description: Given two figures, use the definition of congruence in terms of rigid motions to decide if they are congruent.
description_plain: Your child can decide if two shapes are exactly the same by using movements like sliding, turning, or flipping.

subject: Math
description: Prove the addition and subtraction formulas for sine, cosine, and tangent and use them to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can prove and use formulas for adding and subtracting sine, cosine, and tangent to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Prove the Pythagorean identity sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) = 1 and use it to find sin(θ), cos(θ), or tan(θ) given sin (θ), cos(θ), or tan(θ) and the quadrant.
description_plain: Your child can prove the Pythagorean identity for sine and cosine and use it to find missing trigonometric values based on the angle's location.

subject: Math
description: Use inverse functions to solve trigonometric equations that arise in modeling contexts; evaluate the solutions using technology, and interpret them in terms of the context.
description_plain: Your child can solve trigonometry problems by using inverse functions and technology, and explain what the answers mean.

subject: Math
description: Understand that restricting a trigonometric function to a domain on which it is always increasing or always decreasing allows its inverse to be constructed.
description_plain: Your child understands that limiting a trig function to a certain part lets you find its inverse function.

subject: Math
description: Choose trigonometric functions to model periodic phenomena with specified amplitude, frequency, and midline.
description_plain: Your child can choose trig functions to model repeating patterns with specific height, speed, and middle value.

subject: Math
description: Use the unit circle to explain symmetry (odd and even) and periodicity of trigonometric functions.
description_plain: Your child can use the unit circle to explain patterns and symmetry in trig functions.

subject: Math
description: Use special triangles to determine geometrically the values of sine, cosine, tangent for π/3, π/4, and π/6, use the unit circle to express the values of sine, cosine, and tangent for π - x, π + x, and 2π - x in terms of their values for x, where x is any real number.
description_plain: Your child can find sine, cosine, and tangent values for special angles using triangles and the unit circle.

subject: Math
description: Explain how the unit circle in the coordinate plane enables the extension of trigonometric functions to all real numbers, interpreted as radian measures of angles traversed counterclockwise around the unit circle.
description_plain: Your child understands how the unit circle helps extend trig functions to all angles measured in radians.

subject: Math
description: Understand radian measure of an angle as the length of the arc on the unit circle subtended by the angle.
description_plain: Your child understands that the radian measure of an angle is the length of the arc it cuts out on a circle with a radius of one.

subject: Math
description: Construct exponential functions, given a graph, a description of a relationship, or two input-output pairs (include reading these from a table).
description_plain: Your child can create exponential functions from graphs, descriptions, or tables showing input and output values.

subject: Math
description: Recognize situations in which a quantity grows or decays by a constant percent rate per unit interval relative to another.
description_plain: Your child can recognize when something grows or shrinks by the same percent over equal time periods.

subject: Math
description: Recognize situations in which one quantity changes at a constant rate per unit interval relative to another.
description_plain: Your child can recognize when something changes by the same amount over equal time periods.

subject: Math
description: Prove that linear functions grow by equal differences over equal intervals, and that exponential functions grow by equal factors over equal intervals.
description_plain: Your child can explain that linear functions increase by the same amount over equal steps, while exponential functions increase by the same factor.

subject: Math
description: Distinguish between situations that can be modeled with linear functions and with exponential functions.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between situations best described by linear growth and those best described by exponential growth.

subject: Math
description: Understand the inverse relationship between exponents and logarithms and use this relationship to solve problems involving logarithms and exponents.
description_plain: Your child understands that exponents and logarithms undo each other and can use this to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Produce an invertible function from a non-invertible function by restricting the domain.
description_plain: Your child can make a function reversible by limiting the inputs it uses.

subject: Math
description: Verify by composition that one function is the inverse of another.
description_plain: Your child can check that two functions undo each other by combining them.

subject: Math
description: Read values of an inverse function from a graph or a table, given that the function has an inverse.
description_plain: Your child can find values of an inverse function by looking at its graph or table.

subject: Math
description: Write an expression for the inverse of a function.
description_plain: Your child can write an expression for the inverse of a function.

subject: Math
description: Find inverse functions.
description_plain: Find inverse functions.

subject: Math
description: Transform parent functions (f(x)) by replacing f(x) with f(x)+k, kf(x), f(kx), and f(x+k) for specific values of k (both positive and negative); find the value of k given the graphs. Experiment with cases and illustrate an explanation of the effects on the graph using technology. Include recognizing even and odd functions from their graphs and algebraic expressions for them.
description_plain: Your child can change basic function graphs by adding, multiplying, or shifting them and use technology to see how these changes affect the graph.

subject: Math
description: Write arithmetic and geometric sequences and series both recursively and with an explicit formula, use them to model situations, and translate between the two forms.
description_plain: Your child can write number patterns using formulas and use them to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Compose functions.
description_plain: Compose functions.

subject: Math
description: Determine an explicit expression , a recursive function, or steps for calculation from a context.
description_plain: Your child can write formulas or steps to calculate values based on a real-life situation.

subject: Math
description: Use functions to model real-world relationships.
description_plain: Your child can use functions to describe and solve real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Compare properties of two functions using a variety of representations (algebraically, graphically, numerically in tables, or by verbal descriptions).
description_plain: Your child can compare two functions by looking at their equations, graphs, tables, or descriptions.

subject: Math
description: Use the properties of exponents to interpret expressions for exponential functions.
description_plain: Your child can use exponent rules to understand and explain exponential function expressions.

subject: Math
description: Use the process of factoring and completing the square in a quadratic function to show zeros, extreme values, and symmetry of the graph, and interpret these in terms of a context.
description_plain: Your child can use factoring and completing the square to find important points on a quadratic graph and explain what they mean.

subject: Math
description: Write a function in different but equivalent forms to reveal and explain different properties of the function.
description_plain: Your child can write the same function in different ways to show different features.

subject: Math
description: Graph trigonometric functions, showing period, midline, and amplitude.
description_plain: Your child can draw graphs of trig functions showing their repeating patterns and heights.

subject: Math
description: Graph rational functions, identifying zeros and asymptotes when suitable factorizations are available, and showing end behavior.
description_plain: Your child can graph rational functions, showing where they cross zero, their behavior near certain lines, and how they behave far away.

subject: Math
description: Graph polynomial functions, identifying zeros when suitable factorizations are available, and showing end behavior.
description_plain: Your child can graph polynomial functions, find where they cross the axis when possible, and show how the graph behaves at the ends.

subject: Math
description: Graph piecewise-defined functions, including step functions.
description_plain: Your child can graph functions that are made of different pieces, including step-like graphs.

subject: Math
description: Graph logarithmic functions, emphasizing the inverse relationship with exponentials and showing intercepts and end behavior.
description_plain: Your child can graph logarithmic functions, showing how they are the opposite of exponential functions, including their intercepts and end behavior.

subject: Math
description: Graph square root, cube root, and exponential functions.
description_plain: Your child can graph square root, cube root, and exponential functions.

subject: Math
description: Graph functions expressed symbolically and show key features of the graph, by hand in simple cases and using technology for more complicated cases.
description_plain: Your child can graph functions shown by formulas and identify important features by hand for simple cases and using technology for harder ones.

subject: Math
description: Calculate and interpret the average rate of change of a function (presented symbolically or as a table) over a specified interval. Estimate the rate of change from a graph.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain how fast a function changes on average over a certain range, using tables, symbols, or graphs.

subject: Math
description: Relate the domain of a function to its graph and, where applicable, to the quantitative relationship it describes.
description_plain: Your child understands how the possible input values of a function relate to its graph and the real-world situation it describes.

subject: Math
description: For a function that models a relationship between two quantities, interpret key features of expressions, graphs and tables in terms of the quantities, and sketch graphs showing key features given a description of the relationship. Key features include: intercepts; intervals where the function is increasing, decreasing, positive, or negative; relative maximums and minimums; symmetries; end behavior; and periodicity.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain important parts of a function’s graph and equation, like where it goes up or down, crosses axes, or repeats.

subject: Math
description: Recognize patterns in order to write functions whose domain is a subset of the integers. Limited to linear and quadratic.
description_plain: Your child can find patterns and write functions that use whole numbers as inputs, focusing on lines and parabolas.

subject: Math
description: Use function notation, evaluate functions for inputs in their domains, and interpret statements that use function notation in terms of a context.
description_plain: Your child can use function notation to find outputs for inputs and explain what function statements mean in real situations.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a function from one set (called the domain) to another set (called the range) assigns to each element of the domain exactly one element of the range. If f is a function and x is an element of its domain, then f(x) denotes the output of f corresponding to the input x. The graph of f is the graph of the equation y = f(x).
description_plain: Your child understands that a function matches each input with exactly one output and can read and graph functions using this idea.

subject: Math
description: Solve an equation f(x) = g(x) by graphing y = f(x) and y = g(x) and finding the x-value of the intersection point. Include cases where f(x) and/or g(x) are linear, polynomial, rational, absolute value, exponential, and logarithmic functions.
description_plain: Your child can solve equations by graphing two functions and finding where their lines cross, including many types of functions.

subject: Math
description: Understand that the graph of an equation in two variables is the set of all its solutions plotted in the coordinate plane, often forming a curve (which could be a line).
description_plain: Your child understands that the graph of an equation shows all the solutions as points on a plane, often forming a curve or line.

subject: Math
description: Represent a system of linear equations as a single matrix equation and solve (incorporating technology) for matrices of dimension 3 × 3 or greater.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve systems of linear equations using matrices and technology.

subject: Math
description: Solve quadratic inequalities and identify the domain.
description_plain: Your child can solve inequalities with quadratic expressions and identify the set of possible input values.

subject: Math
description: Use the method of completing the square to transform and solve any quadratic equation in x into an equation of the form (x - p)² = q that has the same solutions.
description_plain: Your child can change any quadratic equation into a form like (x - p)² = q by completing the square and then solve it.

subject: Math
description: Solve quadratic equations with complex solutions written in the form a ± bi for real numbers a and b.
description_plain: Your child can solve quadratic equations that have answers involving imaginary numbers written as a ± bi.

subject: Math
description: Solve quadratic equations and inequalities
description_plain: Your child can solve quadratic equations and inequalities.

subject: Math
description: Solve radical and rational exponent equations and inequalities in one variable, and give examples showing how extraneous solutions may arise. (A.REI.2)
description_plain: Your child can solve equations and inequalities that have roots or fractional exponents and understand when some answers might not actually work.

subject: Math
description: Solve exponential and logarithmic equations.
description_plain: Your child can solve equations involving exponential and logarithmic expressions.

subject: Math
description: Solve rational, absolute value and square root equations. Limited to simple equations such as, 2√x-3 + 8 = 16, x + 3/2x - 1 = 5, x ≠ ½.
description_plain: Your child can solve simple equations with square roots, absolute values, or fractions.

subject: Math
description: Solve equations in one variable and give examples showing how extraneous solutions may arise.
description_plain: Your child can solve one-variable equations and understand why some answers might not actually work.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understanding to solve equations, inequalities, and compound inequalities in one variable, including literal equations and inequalities. (A.REI.3)
description_plain: Your child can solve different kinds of equations and inequalities, including those with letters instead of numbers.

subject: Math
description: Explain each step in solving a simple equation as following from the equality of numbers asserted at the previous step, starting from the assumption that the original equation has a solution. Construct a viable argument to justify a solution method.
description_plain: Your child can explain each step when solving an equation and give reasons why the steps work.

subject: Math
description: Rearrange formulas to highlight a quantity of interest, using the same reasoning as in solving equations.
description_plain: Your child can rearrange formulas to solve for a specific variable, using the same ideas as solving equations.

subject: Math
description: Represent constraints by equations or inequalities, and by systems of equations and/or inequalities, and interpret solutions as viable or non-viable options in a modeling context.
description_plain: Your child can write equations or inequalities to show limits or rules in a problem and understand which solutions make sense.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understanding to create equations in two or more variables to represent relationships between quantities; graph equations on coordinate axes with labels and scales.
description_plain: Your child can create equations with two or more variables to show relationships and graph them with labels and scales.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understanding to create equations and inequalities in one variable and use them to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve equations and inequalities with one variable to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide rational expressions.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide expressions that have fractions with variables.

subject: Math
description: Rewrite simple rational expressions in different forms; write a(x)/b(x) in the form q(x) + r(x)/b(x), where a(x), b(x), q(x), and r(x) are polynomials with the degree of r(x) less than the degree of b(x), using inspection, long division, or, for the more complicated examples, a computer algebra system.
description_plain: Your child can rewrite rational expressions in simpler forms using division or technology.

subject: Math
description: Know and apply the Binomial Theorem for the expansion of (x + y)² in powers of x and y for a positive integer n, where x and y are any numbers, with coefficients determined for example by Pascal's Triangle. The Binomial Theorem can be proven by mathematical induction or by a combinatorial argument.
description_plain: Your child knows how to expand expressions like (x + y)ⁿ using the Binomial Theorem and patterns like Pascal’s Triangle.

subject: Math
description: Generate polynomial identities from a pattern.
description_plain: Your child can create formulas for polynomials by recognizing patterns.

subject: Math
description: Know and apply the Remainder Theorem: For a polynomial p(x) and a number c, the remainder on division by (x - c) is p(c), so p(c) = 0 if and only if (x - c) is a factor of p(x).
description_plain: Your child understands that when dividing a polynomial by (x - c), the remainder is found by plugging c into the polynomial, and if that equals zero, (x - c) is a factor.

subject: Math
description: Factor higher degree polynomials; identifying that some polynomials are prime.
description_plain: Your child can break down polynomials with many terms into factors and recognize when a polynomial cannot be factored.

subject: Math
description: Use the properties of exponents to transform expressions for exponential functions.
description_plain: Your child can use the rules of exponents to rewrite and simplify expressions with exponential functions.

subject: Math
description: Complete the square in a quadratic expression to reveal the maximum or minimum value of the function it defines.
description_plain: Your child can complete the square in a quadratic expression to find its highest or lowest value.

subject: Math
description: Choose and produce an equivalent form of an expression to reveal and explain properties of the quantity represented by the expression.
description_plain: Your child can rewrite expressions in different ways to better understand what they represent.

subject: Math
description: Use the structure of an expression to identify ways to rewrite it.
description_plain: Your child can look at how an expression is built to find ways to rewrite it.

subject: Math
description: Interpret complicated expressions by viewing one or more of their parts as a single entity.
description_plain: Your child can understand complicated expressions by treating parts of them as single pieces.

subject: Math
description: Interpret parts of an expression, such as terms, factors, and coefficients.
description_plain: Your child can explain parts of an expression like terms, factors, and numbers multiplying them.

subject: Math
description: Interpret expressions that represent a quantity in terms of its context.
description_plain: Your child can explain what expressions mean in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Multiply a vector (regarded as a matrix with one column) by a matrix of suitable dimensions to produce another vector. Work with matrices as transformations of vectors.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a vector by a matrix to get another vector and understand matrices as ways to change vectors.

subject: Math
description: Understand that the zero and identity matrices play a role in matrix addition and multiplication similar to the role of 0 and 1 in the real numbers. The determinant of a square matrix is nonzero if and only if the matrix has a multiplicative inverse.
description_plain: Your child understands that zero and identity matrices act like 0 and 1 in addition and multiplication, and when a matrix can be reversed.

subject: Math
description: Understand that, unlike multiplication of numbers, matrix multiplication for square matrices is not a commutative operation, but still satisfies the associative and distributive properties.
description_plain: Your child knows that multiplying matrices doesn’t always work the same way as numbers but still follows some rules.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, and multiply matrices of appropriate dimensions; find determinants of 2 × 2 matrices.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, and multiply matrices when their sizes match, and find the determinant of 2 by 2 matrices.

subject: Math
description: Multiply matrices by scalars to produce new matrices, (e.g. as when all of the payoffs in a game are doubled.)
description_plain: Your child can multiply every number in a matrix by the same number to create a new matrix.

subject: Math
description: Use matrices to represent and manipulate data, (e.g. to represent payoffs or incidence relationships in a network.)
description_plain: Your child can use matrices to organize and work with data, like showing payoffs in a game or connections in a network.

subject: Math
description: Compute the magnitude of a scalar multiple cv using ||cv|| = |c|v. Compute the direction of cv knowing that when |c|v ≠ 0, the direction of cv is either along v (for c > 0) or against v (for c < 0).
description_plain: Your child can find the size and direction of a vector after multiplying it by a number, knowing if it points the same way or opposite.

subject: Math
description: Represent scalar multiplication graphically by scaling vectors and possibly reversing their direction; perform scalar multiplication component-wise, (e.g. as c(v<sub>x</sub>, v<sub>y</sub>) = (cv<sub>x</sub>, cv<sub>y</sub>).)
description_plain: Your child can show multiplying a vector by a number by stretching or flipping it and can multiply each part separately.

subject: Math
description: Multiply a vector by a scalar.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a vector by a number.

subject: Math
description: Understand vector subtraction v - w as v + (-w), where –w is the additive inverse of w, with the same magnitude as w and pointing in the opposite direction. Represent vector subtraction graphically by connecting the tips in the appropriate order, and perform vector subtraction component-wise.
description_plain: Your child understands subtracting one vector from another by adding the opposite vector and can show this on a graph and by components.

subject: Math
description: Given two vectors in magnitude and direction form, determine the magnitude and direction of their sum.
description_plain: Your child can find the size and direction when adding two vectors given their sizes and directions.

subject: Math
description: Add vectors end-to-end, component-wise, and by the parallelogram rule . Understand that the magnitude of a sum of two vectors is typically not the sum of the magnitudes.
description_plain: Your child can add vectors in different ways and knows that the length of the sum isn’t just the sum of lengths.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract vectors.
description_plain: Add and subtract vectors.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving velocity and other quantities that can be represented by vectors.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems involving velocity and other quantities that have both size and direction.

subject: Math
description: Find the components of a vector by subtracting the coordinates of an initial point from the coordinates of a terminal point.
description_plain: Your child can find the parts of a vector by subtracting the starting point coordinates from the ending point coordinates.

subject: Math
description: Recognize vector quantities as having both magnitude and direction. Represent vector quantities by directed line segments, and use appropriate symbols for vectors and their magnitudes (e.g., v, |v|, ||v||, v).
description_plain: Your child understands that vectors have size and direction and can draw and label them correctly.

subject: Math
description: Know the Fundamental Theorem of Algebra; show that it is true for quadratic polynomials.
description_plain: Your child knows the Fundamental Theorem of Algebra and can show it works for quadratic equations.

subject: Math
description: Extend polynomial identities to the complex numbers.
description_plain: Your child can extend rules about polynomials to include complex numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve quadratic equations with real coefficients that have complex solutions.
description_plain: Your child can solve quadratic equations with real numbers even when the answers are complex numbers.

subject: Math
description: Calculate the distance between numbers in the complex plane as the modulus of the difference, and the midpoint of a segment as the average of the numbers at its endpoints.
description_plain: Your child can find the distance between two points on the complex plane and find the midpoint between them by averaging their coordinates.

subject: Math
description: Represent addition, subtraction, multiplication, and conjugation of complex numbers geometrically on the complex plane; use properties of this representation for computation.
description_plain: Your child can show adding, subtracting, multiplying, and conjugating complex numbers as movements on the complex plane and use these pictures to help with calculations.

subject: Math
description: Represent complex numbers on the complex plane in rectangular and polar form (including real and imaginary numbers), and explain why the rectangular and polar forms of a given complex number represent the same number.
description_plain: Your child can write complex numbers in both rectangular form (using real and imaginary parts) and polar form (using distance and angle) and explain why these two forms represent the same number.

subject: Math
description: Use conjugates to find moduli and quotients of complex numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use conjugates to find the size (modulus) of complex numbers and to divide complex numbers.

subject: Math
description: Find the conjugate of a complex number.
description_plain: Your child can find the conjugate of a complex number, which changes the sign of its imaginary part.

subject: Math
description: Use the relation i² = -1 and the commutative, associative, and distributive properties to add, subtract, and multiply complex numbers.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, and multiply complex numbers using the rule that i squared equals -1 and basic math properties.

subject: Math
description: Know there is a complex number i such that i² = -1, and every complex number has the form a + bi with a and b real.
description_plain: Your child understands that the number i is defined so that i squared is -1, and that any complex number can be written as a + bi, where a and b are real numbers.

subject: Math
description: Choose a level of accuracy appropriate to limitations on measurement when reporting quantities.
description_plain: Your child can decide how exact a measurement needs to be when reporting quantities.

subject: Math
description: Define appropriate quantities for the purpose of descriptive modeling.
description_plain: Your child can choose which quantities to use when describing a situation with math models.

subject: Math
description: Use units as a way to understand problems and to guide the solution of multi-step problems; choose and interpret units consistently in formulas; choose and interpret the scale and the origin in graphs and data displays.
description_plain: Your child uses units to understand problems and solve multi-step questions, picking and interpreting units and scales correctly in formulas and graphs.

subject: Math
description: Rewrite expressions involving radicals and rational exponents using the properties of exponents.
description_plain: Your child can rewrite expressions with roots and fractional exponents by using the rules of exponents.

subject: Math
description: Explain how the definition of the meaning of rational exponents follows from extending the properties of integer exponents to those values, allowing for a notation for radicals in terms of rational exponents.
description_plain: Your child understands that rational exponents are another way to write roots, based on extending the rules for whole number exponents.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the best tools to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics
description_plain: Model with mathematics

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will use their understanding of choices and consequences to make a claim or advance a thesis using evidence and argument.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about choices and their results to make a statement and support it with reasons.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will investigate and connect examples of choices and consequences with contemporary issues.
description_plain: Your child can explore examples of choices and their results and connect them to issues happening today.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will analyze the context and draw conclusions about choices and consequences.
description_plain: Your child can look at situations and figure out what choices were made and what happened because of them.
