subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading, and read alouds.
description_plain: Your child uses new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and being read to.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete sentences in shared language activities.
description_plain: Your child makes and adds to complete sentences during group language activities.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring prepositions in written work (e.g., to, from, in, out, on, off, for, of, by, with).
description_plain: Your child uses common small words like to, from, in, and on when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand and use question words (interrogatives) (e.g., who, what, where, when, why, how).
description_plain: Your child understands and uses question words like who, what, where, when, why, and how.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form regular plural nouns orally by adding /s/ or /es/ (e.g., dog, dogs; wish, wishes).
description_plain: Your child says plural words by adding sounds like /s/ or /es/, such as dogs or wishes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring nouns and verbs in speech.
description_plain: Your child uses common nouns and action words when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standards English grammar and usage when speaking.
description_plain: Your child speaks using correct grammar and word use for their age.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak with appropriate volume, enunciation, and rate to express thoughts, feelings, and ideas clearly.
description_plain: Your child speaks clearly with the right volume and speed to share their thoughts and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to supply additional detail to descriptions.
description_plain: Your child adds drawings or pictures to help explain what they are describing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use details to describe familiar people, places, things or events with prompting and support.
description_plain: Your child uses details to talk about people, places, things, or events with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to seek help, get information or clarify what is not understood.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to get help or understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Confirm sequence of events of a read aloud or media presentation by asking and answering questions about key details or requesting clarification of what is not understood.
description_plain: Your child checks the order of events in a story or video by asking and answering questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Extend a conversation through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child keeps a conversation going by talking back and forth several times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., listening to others and taking turns speaking about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child follows rules for talking with others, like listening and taking turns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups to expand language comprehension.
description_plain: Your child joins conversations with different people to talk about topics and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in third grade.
description_plain: Begins in third grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell simple words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of letter-sound relationships.
description_plain: Your child spells simple words by sounding them out using letter sounds they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write a letter or letters for most consonant and short vowel sounds.
description_plain: Your child writes letters for most consonant and short vowel sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name end punctuation.
description_plain: Your child recognizes and names punctuation marks at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize the first word in a sentence and the pronoun I.
description_plain: Your child capitalizes the first word in a sentence and the word I.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete sentences in shared language activities.
description_plain: Your child makes and adds to complete sentences during shared writing activities.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring prepositions in written work (e.g., to, from, in, out, on, off, for, of, by, with).
description_plain: Your child uses common small words like to, from, in, and on in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand and use question words (interrogatives) (e.g., who, what, where, when, why, how).
description_plain: Your child understands and uses question words like who, what, where, when, why, and how in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring nouns and verbs in writing.
description_plain: Your child uses common nouns and action words when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly form upper and lowercase letters with line awareness.
description_plain: Your child writes uppercase and lowercase letters correctly on the lines.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standards English grammar and usage when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word use when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in fourth grade.
description_plain: Begins in fourth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child can remember or find information from experiences or sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of books by a favorite author and express opinions about them).
description_plain: Your child takes part in group research and writing projects, like reading books by a favorite author and sharing opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools with help from adults to create and share writing, sometimes working with friends, and can write by printing letters, writing in cursive, or typing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, respond to questions and suggestions from peers and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: Your child can listen to questions and ideas from friends and add more details to their writing with adult help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in third grade.
description_plain: Begins in third grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating and writing to narrate a single event or several loosely linked events, tell about the events in the order in which they occurred and provide a reaction to what happened.
description_plain: Your child uses drawing, talking, and writing to tell about one or more events in order and shares how they feel about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating and writing to compose informative/explanatory texts in which they name what they are writing about and supply some information about the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses drawing, talking, and writing to give information about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating and writing to compose opinion pieces in which they tell a reader the topic or the name of the book they are writing about and state an opinion or preference about the topic or book (e.g., My favorite book is ...).
description_plain: Your child uses drawing, talking, and writing to say their opinion about a topic or book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in individual or group readings of informational text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child listens to or reads informational texts carefully to understand the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs describing the same general action (e.g., walk, march, strut, prance) by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child shows the difference between similar action words by acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at school that are colorful).
description_plain: Your child connects words to real-life places and experiences, like noticing colorful spots at school.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of frequently occurring verbs and adjectives by relating them to their opposites (antonyms).
description_plain: Your child understands common verbs and adjectives by learning their opposites.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort common objects into categories (e.g., shapes, foods) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child sorts everyday objects into groups like shapes or foods to understand categories.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child explores how words relate to each other and their different meanings with adult help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring inflectional endings and affixes (e.g., -ed, -s, re-, un-, pre-, -ful, -less) to determine the meaning of an unknown word.
description_plain: Your child uses common word endings and beginnings (like -ed, -s, re-, un-) to figure out new word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify new meanings for familiar words and apply them accurately (e.g., knowing duck is a bird and learning the verb duck).
description_plain: Your child can learn new meanings for words they already know and use them correctly, like knowing 'duck' is a bird and also a verb.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases in reading and content to expand language comprehension.
description_plain: Your child will figure out the meaning of new or tricky words and phrases while reading to understand more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in second grade.
description_plain: Begins in second grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic (e.g., in illustrations, descriptions or procedures).
description_plain: With help, your child can find ways two books about the same topic are alike and different, like in pictures or information.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell why an author gives reasons to support ideas in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the text in which they appear (e.g., what person, place, thing or idea in the text an illustration depicts).
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how pictures in a book show people, places, or ideas from the words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Name the author and illustrator of a text and define the role of each in presenting the ideas or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can name who wrote and who drew the pictures in a book and know what each person does.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the front cover, back cover and title page of a book.
description_plain: Your child can point out the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about words they don’t know in a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: With support, your child can explain how two people, events, or ideas in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell the main topic of a book and share important details about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Listen to high quality drama, prose, and poetry to expand language comprehension.
description_plain: Your child listens to stories, poems, and plays to learn new words and ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs describing the same general action (e.g., walk, march, strut, prance) by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child can act out different verbs that mean similar things, like walk, march, or strut, to understand their differences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at school that are colorful).
description_plain: Your child can notice how words relate to real life, like spotting colorful places at school.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of frequently occurring verbs and adjectives by relating them to their opposites (antonyms).
description_plain: Your child understands common verbs and adjectives by learning their opposites, like big and small.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort common objects into categories (e.g., shapes, foods) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can group everyday objects, like shapes or foods, to understand what those groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore word relationships and nuances in word meanings to expand language comprehension.
description_plain: With adult help, your child explores how words relate to each other and what small differences in meaning they have.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring inflectional endings and affixes (e.g., -ed, -s, re-, un-, pre-, -ful, -less) as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word.
description_plain: Your child uses word endings like -ed or prefixes like un- to help figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify new meanings for familiar words and apply them accurately (e.g., knowing duck is a bird and learning the verb duck).
description_plain: Your child can learn new meanings for words they already know and use them correctly, like knowing 'duck' is a bird and also a verb.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases to expand language comprehension.
description_plain: Your child will figure out the meaning of new or tricky words and phrases to understand more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in second grade.
description_plain: Begins in second grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in familiar stories.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell how characters in stories have similar or different adventures and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable for literature.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to kindergarten literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the story in which they appear (e.g., what moment in a story an illustration depicts).
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how pictures in a story show a moment or part of the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, name the author and illustrator of a story and define the role of each in telling the story.
description_plain: Your child can name who wrote and illustrated a story and explain what each person does to tell the story, with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize common types of texts (e.g., storybooks, poems).
description_plain: Your child can recognize different kinds of texts, like storybooks and poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about words they don’t know in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify characters, settings and major events in a story.
description_plain: With help, your child can name the characters, places, and main events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, retell familiar stories, including key events.
description_plain: With support, your child can retell familiar stories and include the important parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important parts of a story, with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read decodable texts that support instruction.
description_plain: Your child reads simple books that match what they are learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between similarly spelled words by identifying the sounds of the letters that differ (e.g., bat/cat, bat/bit/, bat/bad).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that look alike by listening to the different sounds, like bat and cat.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read common high-frequency words (e.g., the, of, to, you, she, my, is, are, do, does).
description_plain: Your child can read common sight words like 'the,' 'you,' and 'is.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Associate the long and short sounds with common spellings (graphemes) for the five major vowels.
description_plain: Your child knows the long and short sounds for the five main vowels and their common spellings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate basic knowledge of phoneme-grapheme correspondences of predictable consonants.
description_plain: Your child knows the sounds that letters make for common consonants.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to sound out and read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend and segment onsets and rimes (word families) of single-syllable spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can blend and break apart parts of words that rhyme, like word families.

subject: Language Arts
description: Count, produce, blend, and segment syllables in spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can count, say, blend, and break words into syllables.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce the initial, medial vowel, and final phonemes (sounds) ) in three-phoneme (consonant-vowel-consonant or CVC) words. (This does not include CVC ending with /l/, /r/ or /x/.) (Phonemic awareness)
description_plain: Your child can say the first, middle vowel, and last sounds in simple three-letter words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Manipulate (segment, blend or substitute) sounds in the initial, final and medial positions. (Phonemic awareness)
description_plain: Your child can break apart, blend, or change sounds at the beginning, middle, or end of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of phonemes (sounds).
description_plain: Your child understands and works with the individual sounds in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name all upper- and lowercase letters of the alphabet.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name all uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand that words are separated by spaces in print and can point with one- to-one correspondence.
description_plain: Your child knows that words in print are separated by spaces and can point to each word as they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that spoken words are represented in written language by specific sequences of letters.
description_plain: Your child understands that spoken words match specific letters in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow words from left to right, top to bottom and page by page.
description_plain: Your child can follow words in a book from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print is organized and what it looks like.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases, including conjunctions, that have been acquired through conversations, reading and read alouds to signal simple relationships.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases, including connecting words like 'and' or 'but,' to show simple ideas when talking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally produce and expand complete simple and compound declarative, interrogative, imperative and exclamatory sentences in response to prompts.
description_plain: Your child can say and make longer sentences, like statements, questions, commands, and exclamations, when asked.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently-occurring adjectives, conjunctions, articles and prepositions when speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses common describing words, connecting words, and small words like 'a' or 'the' when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs to convey a sense of past, present and future when speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses verbs that show past, present, and future when talking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use personal, possessive, and indefinite pronouns when speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'I,' 'my,' and 'someone' when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in basic sentences when speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in simple sentences when talking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, proper and possessive nouns when speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use common, proper, and possessive nouns when talking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when speaking.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses correct grammar and word usage when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences with appropriate volume, enunciation and rate.
description_plain: Your child speaks in complete sentences with clear volume, pronunciation, and speed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts and feelings.
description_plain: Your child adds drawings or pictures to help explain ideas and feelings when it makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use relevant details to describe people, places, things and events, expressing ideas and feelings clearly.
description_plain: Your child uses details to clearly describe people, places, things, and events, sharing ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says to gather additional information or clarify what is not understood.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about what someone says to learn more or understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text read aloud, information presented orally or through media.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about important details in stories or information read aloud or shown.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to clear up any confusion about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to understand better when something is confusing during talks about stories or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by responding to the comments of others through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child will join conversations by listening and responding to what others say, having back-and-forth talks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child understands and follows simple rules for talking with others, like listening carefully and taking turns to speak.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about topics and texts with peers and adults in small and large groups to expand language comprehension.
description_plain: Your child joins conversations with others about topics and stories in small or larger groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in third grade.
description_plain: Begins in third grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell untaught words phonetically, drawing on phonemic awareness and spelling conventions.
description_plain: Your child spells new words by sounding them out using letter sounds and spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for words with common spelling patterns (e.g., floss rule) and for frequently-occurring irregular words.
description_plain: Your child spells common words correctly, including words with tricky spellings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in dates and to separate single words in a series.
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly in dates and to separate words in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use end punctuation for sentences.
description_plain: Your child uses correct punctuation at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize dates and names of people.
description_plain: Your child capitalizes names and dates correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete simple and compound declarative, interrogative, imperative and exclamatory sentences in response to prompts.
description_plain: Your child writes and expands different types of sentences like statements, questions, commands, and exclamations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently-occurring adjectives, conjunctions, articles and prepositions when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses common describing words, joining words, articles, and prepositions when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs to convey a sense of past, present and future when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses verbs that show past, present, and future actions in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use personal, possessive and indefinite pronouns (e.g., I, me, my).
description_plain: Your child uses pronouns like I, me, and my correctly in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in basic sentences.
description_plain: Your child uses singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in simple sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, proper and possessive nouns when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses common, proper, and possessive nouns when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly produce upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child writes uppercase and lowercase letters correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in fourth grade.
description_plain: Begins in fourth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child can remember or find information from experiences or sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of "how-to" books on a given topic and use them to write a sequence of instructions).
description_plain: Your child will work with others to research and write projects, like reading 'how-to' books and writing steps to do something.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With support, your child can use computers or other tools to write and share their work, learning to print, write cursive, or type.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, focus on a topic, respond to questions and suggestions from peers and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: With guidance, your child focuses on a topic, listens to ideas from friends, and adds details to make their writing better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in third grade.
description_plain: Begins in third grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in which they recount two or more appropriately sequenced events, include some details regarding what happened, use temporal words to signal event order and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write stories that tell about two or more events in order, include details, use words that show time order, and finish the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they name a topic, supply some facts about the topic and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts by naming a topic, giving facts, and ending the writing clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or name the book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply a reason for the opinion and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write their opinion by saying the topic or book, giving a reason for their opinion, and ending their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read and comprehend high quality informational text of appropriate quantitative and qualitative complexity for first grade.
description_plain: With help, your child reads and understands informational texts suitable for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs differing in manner by defining or choosing them or by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child learns the different meanings of similar action words by defining, choosing, or acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their uses.
description_plain: Your child finds real-life connections between words and how they are used.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define words by category and by one or more key attributes.
description_plain: Your child defines words by grouping them and describing their important features.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort words into categories to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child sorts words into groups to understand the ideas those groups represent.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With adult help, your child understands how words relate to each other and their different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify root words (e.g., look) and their inflectional endings (e.g., looks, looked, looking).
description_plain: Your child identifies root words and their endings like -s, -ed, or -ing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use affixes to determine the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses prefixes and suffixes to figure out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context (definition and synonym) to determine the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child uses clues in sentences to understand the meaning of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown words and multiple-meaning words and phrases to expand language comprehension.
description_plain: Your child learns the meanings of new and multiple-meaning words to improve understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in second grade.
description_plain: Begins in second grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic (e.g., in illustrations, descriptions or procedures).
description_plain: Your child can find simple ways two texts about the same topic are alike or different, like pictures or descriptions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the reasons an author gives to support ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the illustrations and details in a text to describe its key ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and details in a text to explain the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between information provided by illustrations or other graphics and information provided by the words in a text.
description_plain: Your child tells the difference between information from pictures and information from words in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., headings, tables of contents, glossaries, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child uses parts of a book like headings, tables of contents, and glossaries to find important facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to help determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a text.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand the meaning of words and phrases in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: Your child explains how two people, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main topic of a text and tell important details about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read and comprehend high quality dramas, prose and poetry of appropriate quantitative and qualitative complexity for first grade.
description_plain: With help, your child reads and understands stories, poems, and plays suitable for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs differing in manner by defining or choosing them or by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child learns the different meanings of similar action words by defining, choosing, or acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their uses.
description_plain: Your child finds real-life connections between words and how they are used.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define words by category and by one or more key attributes.
description_plain: Your child can explain what words mean by grouping them and describing important features.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort words into categories to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can sort words into groups to understand what those groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help, your child understands how words relate to each other and different meanings they can have.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use comparative and superlative adjectives (-er, -est).
description_plain: Your child can use words that compare things, like 'bigger' or 'biggest.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify frequently-occurring base words (e.g., look) and their inflectional endings (e.g., looks, looked, looking).
description_plain: Your child can recognize base words and their different forms, like 'look,' 'looks,' and 'looking.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently-occurring affixes as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses common beginnings or endings of words to help figure out what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context (definition and synonym) as a clue to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from sentences, like definitions or similar words, to understand new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown words and multiple-meaning words and phrases to expand language comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words to better understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in second grade.
description_plain: Begins in second grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in stories.
description_plain: Your child can tell how characters’ adventures and experiences are alike and different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable for literature.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature for this grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use illustrations and details in a story to describe its characters, setting or events.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and story details to talk about characters, places, or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify who is telling the story at various points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is telling the story at different parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between books that tell stories and books that give information, drawing on a wide reading of a range of text types.
description_plain: Your child can explain the big differences between storybooks and books that give facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words and phrases in stories or poems that suggest feelings or appeal to the senses.
description_plain: Your child can find words in stories or poems that show feelings or help imagine senses like sight or sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe character, settings and major events in a story, using key details.
description_plain: Your child describes characters, settings, and important events in a story using key details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories, including key details, and demonstrate understanding of the lesson or central message (main idea).
description_plain: Your child retells stories with important details and explains the main lesson or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the Four-Part Processing model for Word Recognition to affirm or self-correct to increase accuracy in fluent reading.
description_plain: Your child uses strategies to check and fix their reading to read more smoothly and correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read text orally with accuracy, prosody, and appropriate rate.
description_plain: Your child reads out loud with accuracy, expression, and at a good speed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads with a clear purpose and understands what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize diphthongs in single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can hear and read vowel sounds that blend together in one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Reads vowel-r combinations in single-syllable words (e.g., ar, er, ir or ur).
description_plain: Your child reads words with vowel and 'r' sounds like 'ar' or 'er' in one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode hard and soft c and g.
description_plain: Decode hard and soft c and g.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate words, including compound words.
description_plain: Your child recognizes and reads words suitable for their grade, including words made of two smaller words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read words with inflectional endings (e.g., makes, walked, ended, played, going).
description_plain: Your child reads words that have endings showing tense or number, like 'walked' or 'playing.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use vowel patterns to decode two-syllable words by breaking the words into syllables.
description_plain: Your child breaks two-syllable words into parts and uses vowel patterns to read them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge that every syllable must have a vowel sound to determine the number of syllables in a printed word.
description_plain: Your child knows that every syllable has a vowel sound and uses this to count syllables in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know final -e and common vowel team patterns for representing long vowel sounds.
description_plain: Your child knows that a silent 'e' and vowel pairs make long vowel sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can read one-syllable words that follow common spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the phoneme-grapheme correspondences for common consonant digraphs (e.g., sh, ch, wh, th, gh).
description_plain: Your child knows letter pairs like 'sh' or 'ch' and the sounds they make.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding and encoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to read and spell words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally segment single-syllable words into their complete sequence of individual phonemes.
description_plain: Your child can say all the individual sounds in a one-syllable word out loud.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and produce initial, medial vowel and final phonemes in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can hear and say the first, middle, and last sounds in spoken one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally produce single-syllable words by blending phonemes, including consonant blends in the initial and final position.
description_plain: Your child can say one-syllable words by blending sounds together, including blends at the start or end.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in spoken one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of phonemes (sounds), syllables, and spoken words.
description_plain: Your child understands sounds, syllables, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., first word, capitalization, ending punctuation).
description_plain: Your child knows what makes a sentence, like starting with a capital letter and ending with punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Have an understanding of important reading terminology (e.g., word(s), letter(s), beginning of sentence, top of the page, bottom of the page).
description_plain: Your child understands basic reading words like letter, word, and knows where to start reading on a page.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate book orientation knowledge.
description_plain: Your child knows how to hold and use a book correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print works and the basic parts of books and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use phrases and words, including adjective and adverbs to describe, which have been acquired through conversations, reading, read alouds and text responses.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases, including describing words, learned from talking, reading, and listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete simple and compound sentences.
description_plain: Your child can make complete simple and joined sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context-appropriate adjectives and adverbs.
description_plain: Your child uses describing words that fit the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use past tense of frequently-occurring irregular verbs (e.g., hid)
description_plain: Your child uses past tense forms of common irregular verbs, like 'hid.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reflexive pronouns (e.g., ourselves).
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'ourselves' to talk about themselves and others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use frequently-occurring irregular plural nouns (e.g., teeth).
description_plain: Your child uses irregular plural nouns, like 'teeth,' correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use collective nouns when speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use group names like 'team' or 'family' when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when speaking.
description_plain: Your child will speak using correct grammar and word usage.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation, including requested detail or clarification and proper usage of English grammar.
description_plain: Your child can say complete sentences with details and correct grammar when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create audio recordings of stories or poems; add drawings or other visual displays to stories or recounts of experiences when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can make audio recordings of stories or poems and add drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Tell a story or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking with appropriate volume, enunciation and rate in coherent sentences.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story or share an experience using facts and details, speaking clearly in full sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says to clarify comprehension, gather additional information or deepen understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to better understand what someone is saying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount or describe key ideas or details from a text read aloud, information presented orally or through media.
description_plain: Your child can retell or describe important ideas from stories or information they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask for clarification and further explanation as needed about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask for more explanation when they don’t understand what is being talked about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by linking their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can join conversations by adding comments that connect to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child can follow rules for group talks, like waiting their turn, listening carefully, and speaking one at a time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups to expand language comprehension.
description_plain: Your child joins conversations with others about different topics and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in third grade.
description_plain: Begins in third grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries or other resources to check and fix spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generalize learned spelling patterns when writing words, including compound words, the doubling rule, and e-drop rule.
description_plain: Your child uses spelling rules they have learned when writing words, like how to double letters or drop the 'e'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an apostrophe to form contractions and frequently-occurring possessives.
description_plain: Your child uses apostrophes to write contractions and show ownership.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in greetings and closings of letters.
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly in greetings and closings of letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize holidays, product names and geographic names.
description_plain: Your child capitalizes holidays, brand names, and place names when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child writes using correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce, expand and rearrange complete simple and compound sentences.
description_plain: Your child can write and change simple and compound sentences to make them better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adjectives and adverbs and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child uses describing words and chooses the right ones to describe people, places, or actions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use past tense of frequently-occurring irregular verbs (e.g., told).
description_plain: Your child uses past tense forms of common irregular verbs like 'told'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reflexive pronouns (e.g., myself)
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'myself' correctly when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use frequently-occurring irregular plural nouns (e.g., feet)
description_plain: Your child writes irregular plural nouns correctly, like 'feet'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use collective nouns when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses group names like 'team' or 'family' when writing.

subject: Language Arts
