description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words that describe states of mind or degrees of certainty.
description_plain: Your child can notice small differences in words that describe feelings or how sure someone is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their uses.
description_plain: Your child can find real-life examples of how words are used.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the literal and figurative meanings of words and phrases in context.
description_plain: Your child can tell when words or phrases mean exactly what they say or something more imaginative.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and the different meanings they can have.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries or beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use glossaries or simple dictionaries, in books or online, to find the exact meaning of important words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word to apply to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., company, companion).
description_plain: Your child can use a word they know to help figure out the meaning of a new word with the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known affix is added to a known word. (e.g., happy/unhappy, do/redo).
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of a new word when a prefix or suffix is added to a word they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context (synonym, antonym, definition, examples) to comprehend the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues in a sentence, like similar or opposite words, to understand a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases to expand language comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words to help them understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and observe differences between the conventions of spoken and written standard English.
description_plain: Your child can notice the differences between how people speak and how they write using standard English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when reading.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about language rules when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the themes, settings and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters (e.g., in books from a series).
description_plain: Your child can compare the themes, settings, and plots of stories by the same author about similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable for literature.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to third grade literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific aspects of a text's illustrations contribute to what is conveyed by the words in a story (e.g., create mood, emphasize aspects of a character or setting).
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures in a story help show feelings, characters, or settings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own perspective (point of view) from that of the narrator or those of the characters.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own point of view and that of the narrator or characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to parts of stories, dramas and poems when writing or speaking about a text, using terms such as chapter, scene and stanza; describe how each successive part builds on earlier sections.
description_plain: Your child can talk or write about parts of stories, plays, or poems using words like chapter, scene, and stanza, and explain how each part connects to the ones before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, distinguishing literal from nonliteral language.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when words are used in a literal or a special way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters in a story (e.g., their traits, motivations or feelings) and explain how their actions contribute to the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can describe characters’ traits, feelings, and reasons for their actions, and explain how these actions move the story forward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables, folktales, and myths from diverse cultures; determine the lesson, moral, or central message (main idea) and explain how it is conveyed through key details in the text.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories from different cultures and explain the lesson or main message using important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a story by looking back at the text for answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the Four-Part Processing model for Word Recognition to affirm or self-correct to increase accuracy in fluent reading.
description_plain: Your child can check and fix their reading to make it more accurate and smooth.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read complex prose and poetry orally with accuracy, prosody and appropriate rate.
description_plain: Your child can read harder stories and poems out loud with accuracy, expression, and a good pace.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read complex text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read challenging texts with purpose and understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words (e.g., gnome, climb, neighbor, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode multisyllabic words (e.g., six syllable types, compound words, inflectional endings, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can sound out and read longer words made of several parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common Latin suffixes (e.g., -able, -ation, -ible).
description_plain: Your child can read words that have common Latin endings like -able or -ation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and know the meaning of the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes (e.g., infield, wonderful).
description_plain: Your child knows common beginnings and endings of words and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding and encoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use phonics and word skills to read and spell words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable for third grade.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to third grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable for third grade.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to third grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal precise actions, emotions or states of being and that are basic to a particular topic.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use grade-appropriate academic and topic-specific words that show clear actions, feelings, or states.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English and situations where informal discourse is appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can tell when to use formal English and when informal language is okay.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly use frequently confused words.
description_plain: Your child can correctly use words that are often mixed up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences, recognizing and correcting inappropriate fragments and run-ons.
description_plain: Your child can make complete sentences and fix sentence fragments or run-ons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use prepositional phrases.
description_plain: Your child can create and use prepositional phrases in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Order adjectives within sentences according to conventional patterns.
description_plain: Your child can put adjectives in the right order in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use modal auxiliaries (e.g., can, must) to convey various conditions.
description_plain: Your child can use helping verbs like can or must to show different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the progressive verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can use verb tenses that show ongoing actions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use relative pronouns.
description_plain: Use relative pronouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases to convey ideas precisely.
description_plain: Your child can pick words and phrases that clearly express their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and word choices when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (e.g., presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (e.g., small-group discussion); use formal English when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child knows when to use formal language, like during presentations, and when informal talk is okay, like in small groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add audio recordings and visual displays to presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, sounds, or videos to presentations to help explain main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story or recount an experience in an organized manner, using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share information, tell stories, or describe experiences in an organized way using facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker provides to support particular points.
description_plain: Your child can find the reasons and proof a speaker gives to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase portions of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively and orally.
description_plain: Your child can restate information they hear or see in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can explain their own ideas and understanding after listening to a discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions to clarify or follow up on information and make comments that contribute to the discussion and link to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to understand better and add helpful comments during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child can follow rules during discussions and do their assigned roles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying and uses that knowledge to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups and teacher-led) with diverse partners on fourth grade topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group talks, listen to others, and share their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly, sometimes taking time to research and revise, and other times writing quickly for different purposes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly for their grade and use resources to check spelling when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma before a coordinating conjunction in a compound sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use a comma before words like 'and' or 'but' when joining two sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks to mark direct speech and quotations from a text.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing what someone says or quoting text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct capitalization.
description_plain: Use correct capitalization.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose punctuation for effect.
description_plain: Your child can choose punctuation marks to make their writing more interesting or clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly use frequently confused words (e.g., to, too, two)
description_plain: Your child can use words that sound alike but have different meanings correctly, like 'to,' 'too,' and 'two.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences, recognizing and correcting inappropriate fragments and run-ons.
description_plain: Your child can write complete sentences and fix mistakes like sentence fragments or run-on sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use prepositional phrases.
description_plain: Your child can create and use phrases that show location or time, like 'under the table' or 'after lunch.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Order adjectives within sentences according to conventional patterns.
description_plain: Your child can put describing words (adjectives) in the right order in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use modal auxiliaries (e.g., can, must) to convey various conditions.
description_plain: Your child can use helping words like 'can' or 'must' to show different meanings or conditions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the progressive verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can use verb forms that show ongoing actions, like 'is running' or 'were playing.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use relative pronouns and relative adverbs.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'who,' 'which,' or 'where' to add more information about people or things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases to convey ideas precisely.
description_plain: Your child can pick the best words and phrases to clearly express their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar, spelling, usage when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar, spelling, and word use when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use information from books or articles to help explain or think about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; take notes and categorize information and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child can remember or find information from books or the internet, take notes, organize it, and list where it came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of one page in a single setting.
description_plain: With some help, your child can use computers and the internet to write, share work, and work with others while typing well enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising and editing.
description_plain: With help from adults or friends, your child can plan, change, and improve their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas to fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that fits the story or experience they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child can use clear words and details that help readers picture what happened.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words and phrases to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'then,' 'next,' or 'finally' to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and description to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child can use conversations and descriptions to show what happened and how characters feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene and introducing characters, then tell what happens in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing sentence or section that relates to the information they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and special vocabulary to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within categories of information using words and phrases (e.g., another, for example, also, because).
description_plain: Your child can connect ideas using words like 'for example,' 'also,' or 'because.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, examples, or quotes to explain their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly and group related information in paragraphs and sections; include formatting (e.g., headings), illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic clearly and group related information into paragraphs, using headings or pictures if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing sentence or section that relates to their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons supported by facts and details to explain their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion and create an organizational structure in which related ideas are grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic or text, state their opinion, and organize ideas to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the difference between fact and opinion.
description_plain: Your child understands the difference between facts and opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that share their point of view with reasons and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend high-quality informational text of appropriate quantitative and qualitative complexity for fourth grade.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging informational texts suitable for fourth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of words by relating them to their opposites (antonyms) and to words with similar but not identical meanings (synonyms).
description_plain: Your child can understand words by knowing their opposites and words with similar meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages and proverbs in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain common sayings like idioms, adages, and proverbs when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the meaning of simple similes and metaphors in context.
description_plain: Your child can explain the meaning of simple comparisons like similes and metaphors in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language and how words relate to each other and have different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use books or online tools to find how to say words and understand their exact meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common Greek and Latin affixes to define the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek and Latin word parts to figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (antonym, examples, inferences, definition, synonym) to determine the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses clues like opposite words, examples, or definitions to understand the meaning of words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases to expand language comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words to improve their understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply acquired skills in writing and speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses what they have learned to write and speak clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from two texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from two texts on the same topic to talk or write about it clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented visually, orally or quantitatively (e.g., in charts, graphs, diagrams, time lines, animations or interactive elements on Web pages) and explain how the information contributes to an understanding of the text in which it appears.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain information shown in pictures, charts, or graphs and how it helps the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event or topic; describe the differences in focus and the information provided.
description_plain: Your child can compare a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event and explain how they focus on different details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a text is organized, like by time order, cause and effect, or problem and solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words or phrases in a text relevant to a fourth grade topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words related to grade 4 topics in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain events, procedures, ideas or concepts in a historical, scientific or technical text, including what happened and why, based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child explains what happens and why in history, science, or technical texts using details from the reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text and explain how it is supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how key details support it, and summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says and to make guesses about what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend high-quality dramas, prose and poetry of appropriate quantitative and qualitative complexity for fourth grade.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands stories, plays, and poems that are right for fourth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of words by relating them to their opposites (antonyms) and to words with similar but not identical meanings (synonyms).
description_plain: Your child understands words by knowing their opposites and similar words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages and proverbs in context.
description_plain: Your child can explain common sayings like idioms, adages, and proverbs when reading stories or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the meaning of simple similes and metaphors in context.
description_plain: Your child explains simple similes and metaphors in stories or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language and how words relate and have different meanings in stories and poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses books or online tools to find how to say words and understand their exact meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common Greek and Latin affixes to define the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek and Latin word parts to figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (antonym, examples, inferences, definition, synonym) to determine to determine the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses clues like opposite words, examples, or definitions to understand the meaning of words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases to expand language comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words to improve their understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when reading.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the development of similar themes and topics (e.g., opposition of good and evil) and patterns of events (e.g., the quest) in stories, myths, and traditional literature from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child compares and talks about similar themes and story patterns in myths and stories from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable for literature.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature for fourth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make connections between the text of a story or drama and a visual or oral presentation of the text, identifying where each version reflects specific descriptions and directions in the text.
description_plain: Your child can connect a story or play to its visual or oral version, pointing out where they match.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first- and third-person narrations.
description_plain: Your child can compare different points of view in stories, like first-person versus third-person narration.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between poems, drama and prose and refer to the structural elements of poems (e.g., verse, rhythm, meter) and drama (e.g., casts of characters, settings, descriptions, dialogue, stage directions) when writing or speaking about a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main differences between poems, plays, and stories and talk about parts like rhythm, characters, and dialogue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including those that allude to significant characters found in mythology (e.g., Herculean).
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including those that refer to famous mythological characters, and explain how figurative language makes the text better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe in depth a character, setting or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., a character's thoughts, words or actions).
description_plain: Your child describes characters, settings, or events in stories or plays using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the theme of a story, drama or poem from details in the text; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child finds the main message or theme in a story, play, or poem and can summarize what it is about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says and to make inferences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the Four-Part Processing model for Word Recognition to affirm or self-correct to increase accuracy in fluent reading.
description_plain: Your child uses strategies to check and correct their reading to read more smoothly and accurately.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read complex prose and poetry orally with accuracy, prosody, and appropriate rate.
description_plain: Your child reads harder stories and poems out loud with correct speed, expression, and accuracy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read complex text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads challenging texts carefully and understands what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads smoothly and accurately enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding and encoding words. Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read and spell unfamiliar multisyllabic words accurately in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word parts to read and spell new, longer words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to fourth grade.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to fourth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to fourth grade.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to fourth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal contrast, addition and other logical relationships.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses grade-level words and phrases, including ones that show contrast or add information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correlative conjunctions. (e.g., either/or)
description_plain: Your child uses pairs of connecting words like either/or correctly when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb tense.
description_plain: Your child notices and fixes mistakes when verb tenses change incorrectly in speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verb tense to convey various times, sequences, states and conditions.
description_plain: Your child uses verb tenses to show different times, order of events, and conditions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the perfect verb tenses (e.g., I had walked, I have walked)
description_plain: Your child forms and uses perfect verb tenses like 'had walked' or 'have walked' when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine and reduce sentences for meaning, read/listener interest and style.
description_plain: Your child changes sentences by adding, combining, or shortening them to make their meaning and style better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when speaking.
description_plain: Your child speaks using correct grammar and language rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, using formal English when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation, using formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components (e.g., graphics, sound) and visual displays in presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, sounds, or other media to presentations to help explain main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text or present an opinion, sequencing ideas logically and using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can report on a topic or share an opinion clearly, using facts and details in order, and speak at a pace that is easy to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the points a speaker makes and explain how each claim is supported by reasons and evidence.
description_plain: Your child summarizes what a speaker says and explains how the speaker supports their ideas with reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize a written text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively and orally.
description_plain: Your child can summarize information from a text read aloud or from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and draw conclusions in light of information and knowledge gained from the discussions.
description_plain: Your child understands the main ideas shared in a discussion and can make conclusions based on what was talked about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions by making comments that contribute to the discussion and elaborate on the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer specific questions during a discussion and add to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child follows the agreed rules during discussions and does the roles they are assigned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying the topic and uses that knowledge to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups and teacher-led) with diverse partners on fifth grade topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child participates well in different kinds of discussions, listens to others, and shares their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, reasons, and readers, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting reference materials as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly for their grade and use resources like dictionaries when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use underlining, quotation marks or italics to indicate titles of works.
description_plain: Your child knows how to show titles of books, stories, or other works by underlining, using quotation marks, or italics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to set off the words yes and no (e.g., Yes, thank you), to set off a tag question from the rest of the sentence (e.g., It's true, isn't it?) and to indicate direct address (e.g., Is that you, Sam?)
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly with words like yes and no, in questions added to sentences, and when speaking directly to someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate an introductory element from the rest of the sentence.
description_plain: Your child uses a comma after introductory words or phrases at the beginning of a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation to separate items in a series.
description_plain: Your child uses commas to separate items in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correlative conjunctions. (e.g., either/or)
description_plain: Your child uses pairs of connecting words like either/or to join ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb tense.
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix mistakes when verb tenses change incorrectly in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verb tense to convey various times, sequences, states and conditions.
description_plain: Your child uses verb tenses to show when things happen and how events are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the perfect verb tenses (e.g., I had walked, I have walked)
description_plain: Your child can form and use perfect verb tenses like 'had walked' or 'have walked' correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of conjunctions, prepositions and interjections in general and their function in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands what conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections do in sentences and can explain their use.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine and reduce sentences for meaning, reader/listener interest and style.
description_plain: Your child can make sentences longer, shorter, or combine them to make writing clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply fifth grade reading standards to informational texts (e.g., "Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text, identifying which reasons and evidence support which point[s]").
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support points in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply fifth grade reading standards to literature (e.g., "Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings or events in a story or a drama, drawing on specific details in the text [e.g., how characters interact]").
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast characters, settings, or events in stories or plays using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection and research.
description_plain: Your child uses information from stories or nonfiction texts to support their thinking, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; summarize or paraphrase information in notes and finished work and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child remembers or finds important information from experiences, books, or the internet, summarizes it, and lists where it came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that use several sources to build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects using several sources to learn about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of two pages in a single sitting.
description_plain: With some help, your child uses technology like the internet to write, share their work, and work with others, while showing good keyboard skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways with help from adults and peers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and organizes their ideas to fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows naturally from the story or events they describe.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child uses clear words and details that appeal to the senses to describe experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words, phrases and clauses to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child uses different words and phrases to show the order of events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, description and pacing, to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses storytelling techniques like dialogue and description to show what happens and how characters feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing characters, then tells events in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined experiences using good techniques, details, and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a closing statement that relates to the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words and special vocabulary to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within and across categories of information using words, phrases and clauses (e.g., in contrast, especially).
description_plain: Your child connects ideas within and between categories using words like 'in contrast' or 'especially.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, provide a general observation and focus and group related information logically; include formatting (e.g., headings), illustrations and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly, shares a main idea, and organizes related information logically, using headings or pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic and share ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a closing statement that relates to the opinion they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words, phrases and clauses (e.g., consequently, specifically).
description_plain: Your child connects their opinion and reasons using words like 'consequently' or 'specifically.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide logically ordered reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons in a logical order and supports them with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion and create an organizational structure in which ideas are logically grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child clearly introduces a topic or text, states their opinion, and organizes ideas to support their point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the difference between fact and opinion.
description_plain: Your child understands the difference between facts and opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information.
description_plain: Your child writes opinion pieces on topics or texts, giving reasons and information to support their view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend high-quality informational text of appropriate quantitative and qualitative complexity for fifth grade.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands challenging nonfiction texts appropriate for fifth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationships between particular words (e.g., synonyms) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses relationships between words, like synonyms, to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child recognizes and explains common sayings, proverbs, and wise phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language, including similes and metaphors, in context.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains figurative language like similes and metaphors in context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use books or online tools to find how to say words correctly and understand their exact meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots to define the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what a word means by looking at common Greek and Latin parts in the word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (antonym, examples definition, synonyms inferences) to determine the meaning of a word or phrase..
description_plain: Your child can use clues around a word, like opposite words or examples, to understand what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on firth grade reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words using different helpful strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when reading.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from several texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from different books or articles about the same topic and use it to talk or write clearly about that topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text, identifying which reasons and evidence support which point(s).
description_plain: Your child understands how an author uses reasons and facts to support their ideas and can point out which reasons go with which ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw on information from multiple print or digital sources, demonstrating the ability to locate an answer to a question quickly or to solve a problem efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can find answers or solve problems quickly by using information from several books, websites, or other sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple accounts of the same event or topic, noting important similarities and differences in the perspective (viewpoint) they represent.
description_plain: Your child can look at different stories about the same event and notice important similarities and differences in how people see it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/ solution) of events, ideas, concepts or information in two or more texts.
description_plain: Your child can find similarities and differences in how two or more texts are organized, like by time order or cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a fifth grade topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words related to school subjects or topics they are learning about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships or interactions between two or more individuals, events, ideas or concepts in a historical, scientific or technical text based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, or ideas are connected in history, science, or technical texts using details from the reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more main ideas of a text and explain how they are supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how details support them, and tell the main points in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can quote parts of a text accurately when explaining what it says and when making guesses based on the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend high-quality dramas, prose and poetry of appropriate quantitative and qualitative complexity for fifth grade.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand plays, stories, and poems that are just right for fifth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationships between particular words (e.g., synonyms) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use the relationship between words, like synonyms, to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain common sayings and proverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language, including similes and metaphors, in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain comparisons like similes and metaphors in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language and how words relate to each other and have different shades of meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use books or online tools to find how to say words correctly and understand their exact meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots to define the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what a word means by looking at common Greek and Latin parts in the word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (synonym, antonym, definition, examples, inferences) to determine the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues around a word, like synonyms or examples, to understand what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases to expand language comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words to improve their understanding of language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the varieties of English (e.g., dialects, registers) used in stories, dramas or poems.
description_plain: Your child can notice and compare different types of English used in stories, plays, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when reading.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast stories in the same genre (e.g., mysteries and adventure stories) on their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare stories that are the same type, like mysteries or adventures, and talk about how they handle similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable for literature.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature for fifth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how visual and multimedia elements contribute to the meaning, tone or beauty of a text (e.g., graphic novel, multimedia presentation of fiction, folktale, myth, poem).
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures and multimedia help show the meaning, feeling, or beauty of a story, poem, or play.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a narrator's or speaker's point of view influences how events are described.
description_plain: Your child understands how the narrator’s or speaker’s point of view changes the way events are described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a series of chapters, scenes or stanzas fits together to provide the overall structure of a particular story, drama or poem.
description_plain: Your child can explain how chapters, scenes, or parts of a story, play, or poem fit together to make the whole.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative language such as metaphors and similes.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when they are used in a special way like similes, metaphors, or idioms.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings or events in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., how characters interact).
description_plain: Your child can compare characters, places, or events in a story or play using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the theme of a story, drama, or poem , including how characters respond to challenges or how the speaker in a poem reflects upon a topic; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or theme of a story, play, or poem and summarize what happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can quote parts of a story or poem accurately when explaining what it says and when making guesses based on the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the Four-Part Processing model for Word Recognition to affirm or self-correct to increase accuracy in fluent reading.
description_plain: Your child can check and fix their reading mistakes to read more smoothly and accurately.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read complex prose and poetry orally with accuracy, prosody, and appropriate rate.
description_plain: Your child can read difficult stories and poems out loud with correct speed, expression, and accuracy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read complex text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read challenging texts carefully and understand what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words. Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read unfamiliar multisyllabic words accurately in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use sounds, word parts, and syllables to read new, longer words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to fifth grade.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to fifth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to fifth grade.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to fifth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new academic and subject-specific words to understand and explain ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain consistency in style and tone.
description_plain: Your child keeps their speaking style and tone steady and appropriate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize variations from standard English in their own and others' speaking and identify and use strategies to improve expression in conventional language.
description_plain: Your child notices when they or others speak differently from standard English and learns ways to speak more clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct vague pronouns.
description_plain: Your child can find and fix unclear pronouns in their speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in pronoun number and person.
description_plain: Your child can spot and correct when pronouns don’t match in number or person.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use intensive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'myself' and 'ourselves' correctly when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary sentence patterns for meaning, reader/listener interest and style.
description_plain: Your child changes sentence patterns to make their speaking more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components (e.g., graphics, images, music, sound) and visual displays in presentations to clarify information.
description_plain: Your child adds pictures, music, or other media to presentations to help explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, sequencing ideas logically and using pertinent descriptions, facts and details to accentuate main ideas or themes; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can share ideas clearly and in order, using details and speaking loudly and clearly with good eye contact.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child identifies a speaker’s main argument and knows which claims have good reasons and evidence and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how it contributes to a topic, text or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child understands information presented in different ways, like pictures or numbers, and explains how it relates to the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and demonstrate understanding of multiple perspectives through reflection and paraphrasing.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain different viewpoints by summarizing what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions with elaboration and detail by making comments that contribute to the topic, text or issue under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer detailed questions to add to a discussion about a topic or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, set specific goals and deadlines and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow rules during group talks, set clear goals and deadlines, and decide who does what.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying, and uses facts from that preparation to ask questions and think about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups and teacher-led) with diverse partners on sixth grade topics, texts and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different kinds of group talks with classmates and teachers, listen to others' ideas, and share their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Spell correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (commas, parentheses, dashes) to set off nonrestrictive/parenthetical elements.
description_plain: Your child uses commas, parentheses, and dashes correctly to add extra information in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain consistency in style and tone.
description_plain: Your child keeps the same style and tone throughout their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize variations from standard English in their own and others' writing and identify and use strategies to improve expression in conventional language.
description_plain: Your child notices when writing doesn’t follow standard English and uses ways to make their writing clearer and more correct.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct vague pronouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in pronoun number and person.
description_plain: Your child spots and corrects mistakes when pronouns change incorrectly in number or person.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use intensive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'myself' and 'ourselves' to emphasize something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure that pronouns are in the proper case (subjective, objective, possessive).
description_plain: Your child uses pronouns correctly as subjects, objects, or to show possession.

description_plain: Your child uses different sentence structures to make writing more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of and use knowledge of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection and research.
description_plain: Your child finds and uses evidence from stories or informational texts to support their thoughts and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources; assess the credibility of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and providing basic bibliographic information for sources.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books and websites, decide if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and refocusing the inquiry when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects by using several sources and changing their questions if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of three pages in a single sitting.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and the Internet to write, share their work, and work with others, while typing well enough to do these tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults and peers, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting or trying a new approach.
description_plain: With some help, your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to write.

description_plain: Your child writes clearly and organizes their ideas so the writing fits the task, purpose, and audience.

description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows naturally from the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one-time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child uses storytelling techniques like dialogue and pacing to develop events and characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child starts a story by setting the scene, introducing characters or narrator, and organizing events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that sums up the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes in a formal style when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words to show how ideas and concepts connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations or other information and examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic; organize ideas, concepts and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast and cause/effect; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables) and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic and organizes information using methods like comparing, defining, or showing cause and effect, and uses headings or pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.

subject: Language Arts
