subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the active and passive voice.
description_plain: Your child forms and uses verbs in active voice (doing the action) and passive voice (receiving the action).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs in the active and passive voice and in the conditional and subjunctive mood to achieve particular effects.
description_plain: Your child uses verbs in different voices and moods to create special effects in speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multimedia and visual displays into presentations to clarify information, strengthen claims and evidence and add interest.
description_plain: Your child adds pictures, videos, or other media to presentations to make ideas clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with relevant evidence, sound valid reasoning and well-chosen details; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child presents ideas clearly and confidently, using good reasons, details, and appropriate words, while making eye contact and speaking loudly and clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and relevance and sufficiency of the evidence and identifying when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child explains a speaker’s argument and checks if the reasons and evidence make sense and if anything irrelevant is included.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the purpose of information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and evaluate the motives (e.g., social, commercial, political) behind its presentation.
description_plain: Your child looks at information from different sources and figures out why it was shared, like for social, business, or political reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views in light of the evidence presented.
description_plain: Your child listens to new information from others and changes or explains their own ideas based on the evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that connect the ideas of several speakers and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant evidence, observations and ideas.
description_plain: Your child asks questions that connect ideas from different speakers and responds with relevant facts and thoughts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions and decision-making, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child follows rules for group discussions, helps make decisions, keeps track of goals and deadlines, and knows their role.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or researching and uses evidence to explore and think about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups and teacher-led) with diverse partners on eighth grade topics, texts and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different types of group talks, listens to others, and shares their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Spell correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an ellipsis to indicate an omission.
description_plain: Your child uses ellipses (...) to show when words are left out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (comma, ellipsis, dash) to indicate a pause or break.
description_plain: Your child uses commas, ellipses, and dashes to show pauses or breaks in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb voice and mood.
description_plain: Your child notices and fixes mistakes when verbs change voice or mood incorrectly in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the indicative, imperative, interrogative, conditional and subjunctive mood.
description_plain: Your child forms and uses verbs in different moods like statements, commands, questions, conditions, and wishes in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the active and passive voice.
description_plain: Your child forms and uses verbs in active and passive voice in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of verbals (gerunds, participles, infinitives) in general and their function in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains how verb forms like gerunds, participles, and infinitives work in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs in the active and passive voice and in the conditional and subjunctive mood to achieve particular effects.
description_plain: Your child uses verbs in different voices and moods to create special effects in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection and research.
description_plain: Your child uses information from stories or articles to support their analysis, reflection, or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child gathers information from many sources, uses good search words, checks if sources are trustworthy, and quotes or paraphrases correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects to answer questions they choose, using several sources and asking more focused questions to explore the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas efficiently as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child uses technology like the Internet to write, share ideas clearly, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults and peers, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to better meet the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization and style are appropriate to task, purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and logically with organization and style that fit the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the experiences or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific words, detailed descriptions, and sensory language to show what is happening and help readers feel the experience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases and clauses to convey sequence, signal shifts from one timeframe or setting to another and show the relationships among experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child will use different transition words and phrases to show the order of events, changes in time or place, and how experiences are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, description and reflection, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child can use storytelling tools like dialogue, pacing, description, and reflection to develop events, experiences, and characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child will start a story by setting the scene and point of view, introducing characters or a narrator, and organizing events in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, clear details, and well-organized events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that supports and follows from the information or explanation given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child will write using a formal tone appropriate for the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain or inform about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child will use different transition words to connect ideas and make their writing clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can develop a topic by including relevant facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts and information into broader categories; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables) and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child will clearly introduce a topic, organize information into categories, and use headings, charts, or multimedia to help understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts and information through the selection, organization and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain a topic by choosing, organizing, and analyzing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that supports and follows from the argument they have made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child will write in a formal style suitable for arguments.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to connect ideas and show how claims, counterclaims, reasons, and evidence relate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child will support their claims with clear reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their claims, recognize opposing claims, and organize reasons and evidence in a logical way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments that clearly support their claims with good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend high-quality informational text of appropriate quantitative and qualitative complexity for eighth grade.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging informational texts appropriate for eighth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the feelings or ideas that similar words suggest.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use the relationship between words to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech based on the context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can check and confirm the meaning they first guessed for a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials, both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or other resources, both print and online, to find how to say a word or understand its exact meaning and part of speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots to define the meaning of a word (e.g., precede, recede, secede).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin roots and prefixes to figure out the meaning of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to determine the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the words around an unknown word to figure out what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words or phrases based on eighth grade reading and content.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clarify the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using strategies appropriate for eighth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when reading to aid comprehension of informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses their knowledge of language rules to help understand informational texts while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a case in which two or more texts provide conflicting information on the same topic and identify where the texts disagree on matters of fact or interpretation.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more texts that disagree on facts or ideas and explain where they differ.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound, and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; recognize when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child can find and evaluate the main argument and claims in a text, deciding if the reasons and evidence make sense and are relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using different mediums (e.g., print or digital text, video, multimedia) to present a particular topic or idea.
description_plain: Your child can explain the pros and cons of using different formats like print, video, or multimedia to share information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's perspective (viewpoint) and purpose in a text and analyze how the author acknowledges and responds to conflicting evidence or perspectives (viewpoints).
description_plain: Your child can identify the author's viewpoint and purpose and explain how the author deals with different or opposing ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail the structure of a specific paragraph in a text, including the role of particular sentences in developing and refining a key concept.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a paragraph is organized and how its sentences help develop a main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative and technical meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including figurative or technical meanings, and explain how word choices affect the meaning and tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a text makes connections among and distinctions between individuals, ideas or events (e.g., through comparisons, analogies or categories).
description_plain: Your child can analyze how a text connects or compares people, ideas, or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to supporting ideas; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how it develops, and summarize the text without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use the best evidence from a text to support what it says and the conclusions they make.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend high-quality dramas, prose and poetry of appropriate quantitative and qualitative complexity for eighth grade.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories, poems, and plays appropriate for eighth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the feelings or ideas that similar words suggest.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use the relationship between words to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech based on the context.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

description_plain: Your child can check and confirm the meaning they first guessed for a word or phrase.

description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or other resources, both print and online, to find how to say a word or understand its exact meaning and part of speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots to define the meaning of a word (e.g., precede, recede, secede).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin roots and prefixes to figure out the meaning of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to determine the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the words around an unknown word to figure out what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words or phrases based on eighth grade reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clarify the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using strategies appropriate for eighth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when reading to aid comprehension of literary texts.
description_plain: Your child uses their knowledge of language rules to help understand literary texts while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a modern work of fiction draws on themes, patterns of events or character types from myths, traditional stories or religious works such as the Bible, including describing how the material is rendered new.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a modern story uses ideas, events, or characters from old myths, traditional stories, or religious texts like the Bible, and show how the story makes these ideas new.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable for literature.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the extent to which a filmed or live production of a story or drama stays faithful to or departs from the text or script, evaluating the choices made by the director or actors.
description_plain: Your child can compare a movie or live play to the original story or script and explain the choices made by the director or actors.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how differences in the perspective (viewpoint) of the characters and the audience or reader (e.g., created through the use of dramatic irony) create such effects as suspense or humor.
description_plain: Your child understands how different points of view between characters and the audience create feelings like suspense or humor in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the structure of two or more texts and analyze how the differing structure of each text contributes to its meaning and style.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts are structured and explain how their different structures affect their meaning and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a story, including figurative language, and explain how word choices affect the mood or tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular lines of dialogue or incidents in a story or drama propel the action, reveal aspects of a character or provoke a decision.
description_plain: Your child can explain how specific dialogue or events in a story move the plot forward, show character traits, or cause decisions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to the characters, setting, and plot; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message of a story, explain how it develops through the characters, setting, and events, and give a clear summary of the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use parts of a text to explain what it says clearly and to understand ideas that are not directly stated.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses important academic and subject-specific words correctly when reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

description_plain: Your child uses different types of phrases and clauses to express clear meanings and make their writing or speaking more interesting.

description_plain: Your child can combine and evaluate information from different sources, including media and written texts, to answer questions or solve problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's perspective (viewpoint) and purpose in a text in which the rhetoric is particularly effective, analyzing how style and content contribute to the persuasiveness of the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the effectiveness of the structure an author uses in his or her exposition or argument, including whether the structure makes points clear, convincing, and engaging.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
